title: Win the morning, win the pipeline
dek: Agents do not have a lead problem. They have a first-hour problem.
heroAlt: Win the morning, win the pipeline

blocks.html: Watch a struggling agent's morning. Email first, then a scroll, then an errand, and suddenly it is 1 p.m. and the day's prospecting is a thing that will happen tomorrow. The calendar was empty not because leads are scarce but because the first hour got spent on everyone else's priorities.

Producers run the morning in reverse. The first hour belongs to outbound: new conversations, follow-ups, digital door knocks. Before showings, before email, before anything reactive. Because pipeline is the one task that is never urgent today and always fatal to skip for a month.

blocks.text: Your income in ninety days is decided by what you do before 10 a.m. this week.

items.title: Twenty minutes: new outreach.
items.html: Fresh conversations from your sphere, signals, and content replies. New names in the top of the funnel, daily.

items.title: Twenty minutes: follow-ups.
items.html: Every active client and warm ghost gets their scheduled touch. This is where the signed leases actually come from.

items.title: Twenty minutes: relationships.
items.html: One leasing manager, one past client, one referral partner. The long game, played daily.

blocks.html: Block 9 to 10 a.m. tomorrow, phone on do not disturb, and run the three twenties. Then protect that block like it is a closing appointment, because it is all of them.
The Playbook turns the power hour into a thirty-day installed habit, with a scorecard that keeps it honest.

blocks.lead: The Playbook builds this into a 30-day habit.
blocks.label: Get the Playbook for $20
